english_query: why saying something over and over does not make it true
ugf_response: is because the truth of a thing comes from how it fits with what we see and feel, not from how many times we hear it. when someone says the same words again and again, the mind may start to think those words are known or right. this feeling comes from the way our heads work. if we hear a thing many times, it can become easy for us to understand it, and we may stop checking if it fits with other things we know. but the number of times a thing is said does not change that. imagine we say the sky is green. we look at the sky and see that it is blue. The sky being blue shows that the sky is not green. The many words do not add any new fit with the sky. In another case, if a person says a thing many time, we might still check the thing against real things. The count of times the thing is spoken only adds more sound, not more sign that the thing works. So, repeating a thing said does not make sure that the thing is right; it only makes it stronger in our minds.

First, think about two people who want to give things to each other or work together. They make a promise that one will do something for the other. Now we look at what happens when they know they will see each other again in the future. When this happens, they must decide how much to give and how much to keep. If they will meet again, they might try to get as much as they can right now, even if it means being not good to the other person. They might tell things that are not true to get what they want. This is because they do not care what happens after the first meeting. They do not have to face the other person again.

But, if the two people know they will meet later, the way they act changes. They must think about their name and how others see them. If one person tries to take too much or acts in a bad way, the other person will remember. The next time they meet, the second person might not want to work with the first person, or they might try very hard to show they are good to stay away from. This makes the first person lose out on many good ways to get things in the future?

So, when people know they are part of a group that stays together, they often try to find a way to be good to each other. They try to make sure both sides feel happy about what happened before. They use the thought of the future to make sure they act well today. This way, they both win more over a long time than they would if they only met one time. They give and take not just because they are good people, but because it is the best way to make sure their own life stays good.
